docs.title: 🗄️ Welcome to ArchiveMaster
docs.text:
The smart, lightning-fast desktop application to organize your digital life.

Have you ever dumped thousands of scattered photos, videos, and documents into a hard drive, only to realize it's a complete mess? ArchiveMaster is a native desktop application designed to solve that problem.

It recursively scans any folder you point it to, identifies duplicates with cryptographic precision, and neatly copies or moves your media into beautifully organized, chronological folders (e.g., 2024/Jan/Photos).

docs.title: ✨ Key Features
docs.text:
• Smart Deduplication: Identifies exact file matches using SHA-256 hashing. If it finds duplicates, it safely segregates them into a dedicated duplicates/ folder.
  • Granular Control: Toggle exactly what you want to organize (Photos, Videos, or Others).
  • EXIF Date Extraction: Doesn't just rely on file creation dates; it intelligently reads the metadata embedded inside your photos to figure out exactly when they were taken.
  • Beautiful UI: A modern, native desktop interface built with Fluent Design principles, featuring real-time progress bars, speed metrics, and ETA calculations.
  • Session History: Keeps a persistent log of your past organization runs, detailing exactly how much data was processed and saved.

docs.title: 🚀 Get Started
docs.text: Head over to the Downloads page to get the latest version for Windows or Linux!

docs.title: Building and Downloading ArchiveMaster
docs.text: This guide explains how the automated build pipeline works, where to find your compiled application for both Windows and Linux, and how to compile it yourself on your own machine.

docs.title: ☁️ Downloading the Automated Builds (GitHub Actions)
docs.text: You don't need to build the app manually! Every time code is pushed to this repository, GitHub automatically compiles it for you.

docs.title: How to download your .exe and Linux binaries:
docs.text:
1. Open your repository on GitHub.com.
  2. Click on the Actions tab at the top of the page.
  3. Click on the most recent workflow run under the "CI/CD Pipeline" list.
  4. Scroll down to the bottom of the page to the Artifacts section.
  5. You will see three files available for download:
  6. 📦 ArchiveMaster-Windows-Installer: A standard ArchiveMaster_Setup.exe installer for Windows. It installs the app to Program Files and creates a Start Menu shortcut.
  7. 🗂️ ArchiveMaster-Windows-Portable: A portable .zip folder containing the Windows app. No installation required, just extract and run the .exe.
  8. 🐧 ArchiveMaster-Linux-Portable: A portable archive containing the compiled Linux executable.

docs.title: 💻 Building Locally on Windows
docs.text:
If you want to manually build the Windows .exe and the Setup Installer on your own machine:

1. Install Python Dependencies 

pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install pyinstaller

2. Compile the Portable App Run PyInstaller to bundle the code into a folder: 

python -m PyInstaller --noconfirm --onedir --windowed --hidden-import clr --hidden-import win32timezone --hidden-import PIL --hidden-import PIL.ExifTags --add-data "gui;gui/" --name "ArchiveMaster" app.py
Your compiled portable app will be located in dist/ArchiveMaster/ArchiveMaster.exe.

3. Compile the Setup Installer (Optional) If you want to create the ArchiveMaster_Setup.exe installer: 1. Download and install Inno Setup. 2. Right-click the installer.iss file in this folder and select Compile. Your final setup file will be created in the dist/ folder.

docs.title: 🐧 Building Locally on Linux
docs.text:
If you want to manually build the Linux binary on an Ubuntu/Debian machine:

1. Install System Dependencies The desktop window engine requires GTK development headers. 

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y libgirepository1.0-dev gcc libcairo2-dev pkg-config python3-dev gir1.2-gtk-3.0

2. Install Python Dependencies 

pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install pyinstaller

3. Compile the App Note: Linux uses a colon : as the file separator in PyInstaller instead of a semicolon ;. 

python -m PyInstaller --noconfirm --onedir --windowed --hidden-import clr --hidden-import win32timezone --hidden-import PIL --hidden-import PIL.ExifTags --add-data "gui:gui/" --name "ArchiveMaster" app.py
Your compiled Linux binary will be located in dist/ArchiveMaster/ArchiveMaster.

docs.title: 📝 Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to ArchiveMaster will be documented in this file.

docs.title: 🎉 Initial Release
docs.text: This is the first official release of ArchiveMaster!

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Smart Deduplication: Identifies exact file matches using SHA-256 hashing.
  • Granular Media Control: Toggle between scanning Photos, Videos, or Others.
  • EXIF Date Extraction: Intelligently reads metadata embedded inside your photos for precise chronological sorting.
  • Modern UI: A beautiful native desktop interface built with Fluent Design principles.
  • Session History: Keeps a persistent log of your past organization runs.
  • Automated CI/CD: Fully automated pipeline producing Windows Installers and Linux Binaries.

docs.title: 📥 Downloads
docs.text:
Download the latest version of ArchiveMaster below.

All releases are open-source and automatically compiled via GitHub Actions to guarantee safety and transparency.

docs.title: 🪟 Windows
docs.text: We offer two versions for Windows: a standard Installer (recommended) and a Portable ZIP.

docs.title: 1. Windows Installer (Recommended)
docs.text:
This is the standard Windows Setup executable. It will install ArchiveMaster, create a Start Menu shortcut, and handle everything for you.

  • Download ArchiveMaster_Setup.exe

docs.title: 2. Windows Portable
docs.text:
A standalone .zip file containing the application. No installation is required. Just extract the folder and double-click ArchiveMaster.exe.

  • Download ArchiveMaster-Windows-Portable.zip

docs.title: 🐧 Linux
docs.text:
A standalone .tar.gz archive containing the Linux binary.

  1. Download the archive below.
  2. Extract the contents: tar -xvf ArchiveMaster-Linux-Portable.tar.gz

  3. Run the binary: ./ArchiveMaster

  4. Download ArchiveMaster-Linux-Portable.tar.gz

docs.title: 📦 Older Versions
docs.text: If you need a specific older version, you can browse the GitHub Releases Page to see all historical files.
